forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KY2CWAEKSXR967RZ8A1F6CGQ
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8580'
+title: extras-demo `ignore` subcommand + integration test + README
+---
+## What
+Surface the feature through the repo's living-contract-test convention (the `extras-demo` executable) and document it.
+
+1. New subcommand in `Examples/ExtrasDemo/Sources/extras-demo` (own file, e.g. `IgnoreCommand.swift`, following the existing one-subcommand-per-pillar layout): `extras-demo ignore --file <ignore-file> [--file <ignore-file> ...] <path> [<path> ...]`
+   - Each `--file` loads an `IgnoreProcessor`; multiple files are combined left-to-right with `+`
+   - For each tested path, print one line: the path, `ignored` or `included`, and the verdict's why (the `IgnoreVerdict` description — deciding source:line and pattern, or "no rule matched")
+   - Exit code 0; nonzero with a clear message when an ignore file can't be read
+2. Fixture ignore files under the demo's existing fixture tree (a `.gitignore`-style file plus a second e.g. `review-ignore` file demonstrating override-on-combine)
+3. Integration test in `Tests/FoundationModelsExtrasTests/ExtrasDemoIntegrationTests.swift` following the existing subprocess conventions: run the subcommand against the fixtures, assert on stdout verdict lines including the why text and the combined-override case
+4. Update `README.md` with a short IgnoreProcessor section (construction, `+` combination, `evaluate` with why). Note: `plan.md` describes three pillars; add a one-line note there (or in the README) that IgnoreProcessor is a new addition beyond the original three-pillar plan, so the docs don't silently diverge.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `swift run extras-demo ignore --file <fixture> a.log src/keep.log` prints per-path verdicts with source:line reasons
+- [ ] Two `--file` flags demonstrate `+` combination with the later file overriding
+- [ ] README documents the public API with a compilable example snippet
+- [ ] Unreadable ignore file exits nonzero with the path in the message
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Integration test in `ExtrasDemoIntegrationTests.swift` asserting stdout for single-file, combined-file, and error cases
+- [ ] Full `swift test` green (builds the demo via the existing testTarget dependency)
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

+Surface the feature through the repo's living-contract-test convention (the `extras-demo` executable). Documentation (README/plan.md) is a separate follow-on task.

 1. New subcommand in `Examples/ExtrasDemo/Sources/extras-demo` (own file, e.g. `IgnoreCommand.swift`, following the existing one-subcommand-per-pillar layout): `extras-demo ignore --file <ignore-file> [--file <ignore-file> ...] <path> [<path> ...]`
    - Each `--file` loads an `IgnoreProcessor`; multiple files are combined left-to-right with `+`
+   - Tested paths use the trailing-slash convention for directories (`build/` probes as a directory), passed straight through to `evaluate` — document this in the subcommand's `--help` abstract
    - For each tested path, print one line: the path, `ignored` or `included`, and the verdict's why (the `IgnoreVerdict` description — deciding source:line and pattern, or "no rule matched")
    - Exit code 0; nonzero with a clear message when an ignore file can't be read
-2. Fixture ignore files under the demo's existing fixture tree (a `.gitignore`-style file plus a second e.g. `review-ignore` file demonstrating override-on-combine)
-3. Integration test in `Tests/FoundationModelsExtrasTests/ExtrasDemoIntegrationTests.swift` following the existing subprocess conventions: run the subcommand against the fixtures, assert on stdout verdict lines including the why text and the combined-override case
-4. Update `README.md` with a short IgnoreProcessor section (construction, `+` combination, `evaluate` with why). Note: `plan.md` describes three pillars; add a one-line note there (or in the README) that IgnoreProcessor is a new addition beyond the original three-pillar plan, so the docs don't silently diverge.
+2. Fixture ignore files under the demo's existing fixture tree (a `.gitignore`-style file plus a second e.g. `review-ignore` file demonstrating override-on-combine, and at least one directory-only rule exercised via a trailing-slash probe)
+3. Integration test in `Tests/FoundationModelsExtrasTests/ExtrasDemoIntegrationTests.swift` following the existing subprocess conventions: run the subcommand against the fixtures, assert on stdout verdict lines including the why text, the combined-override case, and a trailing-slash directory probe

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `swift run extras-demo ignore --file <fixture> a.log src/keep.log` prints per-path verdicts with source:line reasons
+- [ ] `swift run extras-demo ignore --file <fixture> a.log src/keep.log build/` prints per-path verdicts with source:line reasons, honoring the trailing-slash directory probe
 - [ ] Two `--file` flags demonstrate `+` combination with the later file overriding
-- [ ] README documents the public API with a compilable example snippet
 - [ ] Unreadable ignore file exits nonzero with the path in the message

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Integration test in `ExtrasDemoIntegrationTests.swift` asserting stdout for single-file, combined-file, and error cases
+- [ ] Integration test in `ExtrasDemoIntegrationTests.swift` asserting stdout for single-file, combined-file, directory-probe, and error cases
 - [ ] Full `swift test` green (builds the demo via the existing testTarget dependency)

 ## Workflow

+## What\nSurface the feature through the repo's living-contract-test convention (the `extras-demo` executable). Documentation (README/plan.md) is a separate follow-on task.\n\n1. New subcommand in `Examples/ExtrasDemo/Sources/extras-demo` (own file, e.g. `IgnoreCommand.swift`, following the existing one-subcommand-per-pillar layout): `extras-demo ignore --file <ignore-file> [--file <ignore-file> ...] <path> [<path> ...]`\n   - Each `--file` loads an `IgnoreProcessor`; multiple files are combined left-to-right with `+`\n   - Tested paths use the trailing-slash convention for directories (`build/` probes as a directory), passed straight through to `evaluate` — document this in the subcommand's `--help` abstract\n   - For each tested path, print one line: the path, `ignored` or `included`, and the verdict's why (the `IgnoreVerdict` description — deciding source:line and pattern, or \"no rule matched\")\n   - Exit code 0; nonzero with a clear message when an ignore file can't be read\n2. Fixture ignore files under the demo's existing fixture tree (a `.gitignore`-style file plus a second e.g. `review-ignore` file demonstrating override-on-combine, and at least one directory-only rule exercised via a trailing-slash probe)\n3. Integration test in `Tests/FoundationModelsExtrasTests/ExtrasDemoIntegrationTests.swift` following the existing subprocess conventions: run the subcommand against the fixtures, assert on stdout verdict lines including the why text, the combined-override case, and a trailing-slash directory probe\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [x] `swift run extras-demo ignore --file <fixture> a.log src/keep.log build/` prints per-path verdicts with source:line reasons, honoring the trailing-slash directory probe\n- [x] Two `--file` flags demonstrate `+` combination with the later file overriding\n- [x] Unreadable ignore file exits nonzero with the path in the message\n\n## Tests\n- [x] Integration test in `ExtrasDemoIntegrationTests.swift` asserting stdout for single-file, combined-file, directory-probe, and error cases\n- [x] Full `swift test` green (builds the demo via the existing testTarget dependency)\n\n## Workflow\n- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.\n\n## Review Findings\nFirst review pass (mcp__sah__review, HEAD~1..HEAD) found 4 doc-comment style findings, all fixed:\n- [x] IgnoreCommand.swift struct doc comment: first line now ends in a period instead of an em-dash\n- [x] IgnoreCommand.swift `files` property doc comment: first line now a standalone sentence ending in a period\n- [x] IgnoreCommand.swift `paths` property doc comment: first line now a standalone sentence ending in a period\n- [x] ExtrasDemoIntegrationTests.swift `run(_:environment:)` doc comment: first line now a standalone sentence ending in a period\n\nAlso caught and fixed during this pass: an accidental `swift format -i -r Sources Tests Examples` run (should have been `Sources Tests` only) reformatted unrelated tracked Examples files to 2-space indent; reverted via `git checkout --`. The new untracked IgnoreCommand.swift file was not reverted by that (git checkout doesn't touch untracked files) and was left 2-space indented; rewritten to match the existing 4-space convention used by StackCommand.swift/RenderCommand.swift.
\ No newline at end of file

Integration test in `Tests/FoundationModelsExtrasTests/ExtrasDemoIntegrationTests.swift` following the existing subprocess conventions: run the subcommand against the fixtures, assert on stdout verdict lines including the why text, the combined-override case, and a trailing-slash directory probe\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [x] `swift run extras-demo ignore --file <fixture> a.log src/keep.log build/` prints per-path verdicts with source:line reasons, honoring the trailing-slash directory probe\n- [x] Two `--file` flags demonstrate `+` combination with the later file overriding\n- [x] Unreadable ignore file exits nonzero with the path in the message\n\n## Tests\n- [x] Integration test in `ExtrasDemoIntegrationTests.swift` asserting stdout for single-file, combined-file, directory-probe, and error cases\n- [x] Full `swift test` green (builds the demo via the existing testTarget dependency)\n\n## Workflow\n- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.\n\n## Review Findings\nFirst review pass (mcp__sah__review, HEAD~1..HEAD) found 4 doc-comment style findings, all fixed:\n- [x] IgnoreCommand.swift struct doc comment: first line now ends in a period instead of an em-dash\n- [x] IgnoreCommand.swift `files` property doc comment: first line now a standalone sentence ending in a period\n- [x] IgnoreCommand.swift `paths` property doc comment: first line now a standalone sentence ending in a period\n- [x] ExtrasDemoIntegrationTests.swift `run(_:environment:)` doc comment: first line now a standalone sentence ending in a period\n\nAlso caught and fixed during this pass: an accidental `swift format -i -r Sources Tests Examples` run (should have been `Sources Tests` only) reformatted unrelated tracked Examples files to 2-space indent; reverted via `git checkout --`. The new untracked IgnoreCommand.swift file was not reverted by that (git checkout doesn't touch untracked files) and was left 2-space indented; rewritten to match the existing 4-space convention used by StackCommand.swift/RenderCommand.swift.
